title: The Language of Stones

features.name: syllables
features.desc-on-touch: you pass stones arranged in patterns that sound like words. the archive does not know if the river placed them or if it taught the river to place them

features.name: etymology
features.desc-on-touch: a tower of water-worn rocks, each one a layer of the word's becoming, readable only if you count upward

features.name: drying-mouth
features.desc-on-touch: the riverbed where words pooled before the water learned to move again. now it's a depression that fills with light instead of current

features.name: fossil-script
features.desc-on-touch: lines of unreadable writing hover above the stones, visible only when the light catches them. they might be the river's own language, or yours, misremembered

features.name: bed-reading
features.desc-on-touch: stacked pages of the riverbed itself—sediment, notation, abandonment—each layer a word the earth is still composing

inscription: what remains when water leaves is the alphabet it learned

file.name: Beach_Surgery_Terminology_Draft_v3.md
file.note: local definitions dissolve into riverbed imagery; the archive cannot determine whether this corruption is unintended or the intended meaning

exits.label: back upstream to the mercy bend
deeper into the bed where language becomes water
downstream toward where the words accumulate
